name: Frank Tiberi
abstract: Frank Tiberi (born December 4, 1928) is an American saxophonist and the leader of the Woody Herman Orchestra. He was born in Camden, New Jersey, United States.

interviews.title: Frank Tiberi interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 30, 2022

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Soph
about_quotes.evidence: Woody’s band I mean — playing slowly, that’s it yeah — one of the first nights we played “Early Autumn” which obviously featured the saxes. And the lead tenor player, a great musician who is still with us, Frank Tiberi.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Soph interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 9, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: John Riley was playing drums who now is playing in the Vanguard Jazz Orchestra sitting in Mel Lewis’ seat, Frank Tiberi of course, Dave LaLama, Ralph’s brother on piano. It was really an incredible band.

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: There's two guys I just want to talk about because they've been with you so long, now. And one is Frank Tiberi. What is it, one decade about now, isn't it? INTERVIEWEE: It's ten years, yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: Frank Tiberi, who was with the band probably the longest of anybody, he was still there in the sax section and he always had advice for every drummer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe LaBarbera interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 15, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: He has the record for being probably next to Frank Tiberi, the guy the most, the fellow that’s been with Woody Herman’s band the longest.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Brignola interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: Frank Tiberi would let it go, we were together and he’d break up and let it go... He thought that was hilarious.
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
